message:
fix(reactor): harden fd registration against reuse and stale deregistration

Both the epoll and io_uring reactor backends had correctness issues when
file descriptors were closed and their numbers reused (e.g., via dup2):

epoll:
- register() now scans existing registrations and rejects an fd that is
  already tracked under a different token, preventing silent corruption
  of reactor bookkeeping when an fd number is recycled
- deregister() is now best-effort: if the kernel-side epoll_ctl(DEL)
  fails with EBADF or ENOENT (fd already closed), the error is swallowed
  and the bookkeeping entry is still removed. Previously this returned an
  error, leaving stale registrations behind
- Tests rewritten: deregister_closed_fd now asserts success; new test
  reused_fd_cannot_register_under_new_token_until_stale_token_removed
  simulates fd-number reuse via dup2 and verifies the full lifecycle

io_uring:
- register() adds the same duplicate-fd guard as epoll
- Adds a pre-flight fcntl(F_GETFD) validity check so an invalid fd is
  rejected before any bookkeeping is inserted
- Wraps submit_poll_add in error handling that rolls back the bookkeeping
  entry if the SQE submission fails
- New test: register_invalid_fd_fails_and_does_not_track_registration
  confirms fd -1 fails with EBADF and leaves registration_count at zero

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
